explicit_text: When my parents first came to this country, they were sponsored by a church in Newton, Massachusetts. They didn’t know any English and faced many challenges adapting to life there. Newton was the place where they began their journey toward independence, learning to navigate simple tasks like grocery shopping. It was in Newton that they encountered Duraflame for the first time, which led to a memorable and humorous experience. This city holds a special place in my family’s story as the starting point of our American life.
implicit_text: When my parents first came to this country, they were sponsored by a church in a suburban city near Boston. They didn’t know any English and faced many challenges adapting to life there. This city was where they began their journey toward independence, learning to navigate simple tasks like grocery shopping. It was there that they encountered a popular fire-starting product for the first time, which led to a memorable and humorous experience. This place holds a special spot in my family’s story as the starting point of our American life.

explicit_text: Duraflame was a confusing discovery for my parents when they first shopped in America. They saw many people buying this small item in a red and yellow wrapper with flames on it and thought it was food. My mom tried to cook it by boiling and frying it, but it never cooked properly. Eventually, a neighbor explained that Duraflame wasn’t food at all, but something used to start fires. Although my parents were embarrassed then, we now laugh about how this mistake showed their early struggles adapting to a new culture.
